market_rules:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for NVIDIA (NVDA) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the NVIDIA (NVDA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

By [Adria Cimino] – Feb 11, 2026 at 10:15AM EST

## Key Points

Nvidia is the world’s top AI chip company, and that has resulted in surging earnings.

Though the AI market is growing, some investors still wonder if Nvidia’s momentum will last.

[We’re bullish on these 10 stocks ›] 

Nvidia stock has climbed more than 1,000% over five years.

Investors in artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have closely followed one stock quarter after quarter: Nvidia ([NVDA] +1.02%). That's because the company has built an AI chip empire and, over the past few years, has proven its ability to stay on top. Nvidia has been generating record earnings thanks to this market position, and as investors piled into the stock, it even became the world's biggest company -- market capitalization roared past $4 trillion last year.

So, it's not surprising that investors watch for any news from Nvidia that may signal what's to come. Analysts predict the AI market will reach into the trillions of dollars in just a few years, and companies from Alphabet to Amazon recently announced increased spending on AI infrastructure. Still, some investors worry about the risk that spending may outpace demand -- and that certain stocks may not be able to live up to expectations, especially considering their valuations.

All of this means investors are likely to focus on something ha

news.headline: Nvidia: Ready To Outperform In 2026 -- Again (Rating Upgrade)
news.content:
Nvidia: Ready To Outperform In 2026 — Again (Rating Upgrade) (NASDAQ:NVDA) | Seeking Alpha

[Millennial Dividends] 

8.91K Followers

5

Share

Play(14min)

- [Comments(20)] 

## Summary

- I see $190/share as an attractive entry, targeting $210/share by mid-2026 and $340/share in 2027 on sustained AI spending momentum.
- AI CapEx is expected to grow 50–60% YoY in 2026, with 39% allocated to GPUs, directly fueling NVDA's top-line and margin strength.
- NVDA's valuation at 41.6x P/E appears justified by projected 56–62% EPS growth through FY27, supporting a potential further expansion.
- Nvidia Corporation remains a core outperformer, driven by dominant AI GPU market share and accelerating AI-related CapEx from major tech firms.

BING-JHEN HONG/iStock Editorial via Getty Images

Nvidia Corporation's ([NVDA]) stock is up 13.4x in the last five years while its revenue grew 6.8x over the same timeframe. If were to zoom out just a bit more, to 10 years, Nvidia's revenue

This article was written by

[Millennial Dividends] 

8.91K Followers

I’m a Financial Analyst at a Fortune 500 company, investing with a long runway—30 years to retirement and plenty of compounding ahead. I write about building a thoughtful portfolio that balances strong growth potential with solid fundamentals. My focus is on high-quality businesses, mostly in the U.S. and Europe—companies with staying power, industry-leading profitability, low leverage, and room to grow. I’m especially interested in portfolio strategy, capital allocation, and what makes a business truly worth holding for the long haul. Investing, for me, is about more than just returns—it’s a way to challenge my thinking, stay curious, and move steadily toward financial independence.

agent_action.reasoning: The current market data shows a 21% probability of NVDA closing above $210 by the end of February. Recent news highlights Nvidia's strong position in the AI market and positive earnings, but the stock is currently trading at $189.82, which is below the target. Given the current price of the market at 0.50, which matches my belief probability, there is no positive expected value in buying at this time. Therefore, I will hold my position.
